What sets us apart is our technology.
It is our data that wins the room.
The reason we win is one differentiator.
All a good pitch needs is one clear message.
So, Doctor, you have three minutes. What makes your clinic different from the incumbent?
What sets us apart is one thing: it is our technology that lowers the cost per cycle. That is our differentiator.
Many founders say that. What I want to see is traction. Do you have any?
We do. What convinced our first partners was the pilot: forty cycles, and one clear use case.
That is compelling. And the pain point you solve?
It is cost. What patients feel most is the price, and that is exactly what we fix.
Good. And what is the upside for me, the return on investment?
The reason this is a standout is the margin. What you get is a model that grows, not a single clinic.
Then you have my buy-in. It is founders like you that I invest in.
It is cost that our patients feel most.
What investors want is traction, and we have it.
The reason to back us is a strong return on investment.
All a great pitch needs is one clear, compelling message.

Hi Eliane, it is your pitch coach. One idea before tomorrow: it is not your slides that win the room, it is your first sentence. What investors remember is the one thing that makes you different, so lead with your differentiator. Do not list ten features. What matters is the single pain point you solve and the traction that proves it. When they ask about money, be clear: what they want to see is the return on investment and the upside. And tailor every line to the person in front of you, because a pitch that resonates with one investor may fall flat with another. Remember: what closes a round is not a perfect pitch deck, it is a compelling story that people believe.

Hi team, it is Eliane. Let me recap how we pitch tomorrow. What we lead with is our differentiator: it is our technology that lowers the cost per cycle. The reason we open there is simple, that is the pain point patients feel most. Then we show traction, the pilot and one strong use case, because what an investor wants is proof, not promises. When we reach the numbers, what we highlight is the return on investment and the upside. I have tailored the pitch deck to James, so it resonates with what he cares about. If we do this well, the standout in the room will be us, and we walk out with his buy-in.
